mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in monthly employment counts signals an accelerating economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates a broad-based collapse in aggregate demand, forcing equity valuations lower as revenue growth expectations are revised downward over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects recovering capacity utilization and stronger corporate throughput, which translates directly into higher earnings per share and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative during periods of accelerating INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding two percent triggers a recessionary earnings revision cycle, causing equity markets to price in sustained profit declines and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward backtest shows positive median forward returns during triggered GDP contractions, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate underlying economic resilience, supporting steady corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month holding period.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX 20-day returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 220.0 while the federal funds rate remains below two percent, real interest rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when real rates are deeply negative, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below eight hundred thousand units signals a credit-driven consumption shock, reducing household wealth and corporate revenues, which forces negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold breach fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 40% of cases, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling period.
